# Nurses' combined-salary tables (dirug achim ve'achayot)

> **READ THIS FIRST. These figures are effective 01/12/2008 and are NOT a nurse's
> salary today.** They are reproduced because they are the last combined-salary
> (sachar meshulav) grid the Wage Commissioner published openly, and because the
> STRUCTURE they encode (rank range, the education-level ladder, the seniority
> curve) is still how the dirug works. The shekel cells themselves have since been
> moved by the 2020-2027 framework agreement and by nurses' agreements that are
> layered on as separate supplements rather than as a republished grid. Never quote
> a cell from this file as a current base. Use it to locate the worker on the ladder,
> then read the live cell from the union or the Wage Commissioner.

Source: Ministry of Finance, Wage and Labour Agreements Division
(`משרד האוצר - האגף לשכר והסכמי עבודה`), `משכורת משולבת בש"ח לחודש לדירוג אחיות`,
`בתוקף מ- 01/12/08`, as published in the local-government service-conditions compendium.

## How to read these tables

1. **Pick the table by education level.** There are five, not one: מעשי, מוסמך, ב.א, מ.א, ד"ר.
2. **Then find the cell** at the worker's rank (רמה/דרגה) and recognized seniority (ותק).
3. **That cell IS the combined salary.** It already includes seniority. Do not add a seniority percentage on top.

Ranks run **10 to 21** in every table. Seniority rows run **0 to 40**.

### The education ladder is an offset, not a separate scale

The five tables are the same value ladder shifted by one rank per education level:
a doctorate-holder at rank N earns what an MA holds at N+1, a BA at N+2, a
registered nurse at N+3, and a practical nurse at N+4. At seniority 0, all five
of these are the same 3,960.7 NIS cell: ד"ר rank 15, מ.א 16, ב.א 17, מוסמך 18, מעשי 19.
So education does not open a different pay scale; it moves the worker up the same one.

### Seniority curve (from the tables' own notes)

The per-year increment is a percentage of the previous seniority year's combined salary:

| Seniority years | Increment per year |
|---|---|
| 1 to 9 | 3.10% |
| 10 to 19 | 2.10% |
| 20 to 32 | 1.90% |
| 33 to 35 | 1.10% |
| 36 and on | 0.85% |

The curve flattens sharply after year 32, which is why a veteran nurse's base
appears to stop moving: that is the schedule, not a payroll error.
